CONTENT.Club career: Dumbravanu made two appearances for Zaria Bălți in the 2018 Moldovan National Division.In 2018 he became a Genoa player.In January 2020 he went to Pescara on loan.He never played a match for Pescara and at the end of the loan he came back to Genoa. He made his debut with the Rossoblù on 13 January 2021 in a Coppa Italia game against Juventus.On 14 August 2021, he joined Lucchese in Serie C on loan. On 29 January 2022, he moved on loan to Siena.On 12 July 2022, Dumbravanu joined SPAL on a permanent deal until 30 June 2025. He was immediately loaned out to Cypriot club APOEL. Having not found any game time in Cyprus, on 31 January 2023 the defender was re-called by SPAL. On 31 January 2024, Dumbravanu was loaned by Messina.
CONTENT.External links: Daniel Dumbrăvanu at FootballDatabase.euDaniel Dumbrăvanu at National-Football-Teams.com
CONTENT.International career: He made his debut for Moldova national football team on 31 March 2021 in a World Cup qualifier against Israel.

SUMMARY: Daniel Dumbravanu (born 22 July 2001) is a Moldovan professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Italian Serie C Group B club SPAL and the Moldova national team.
